 {"id":"bd-1504","title":"Test sync with real repos","description":"**Test sync against real GitHub repos**

## What
Test sync functionality with real public repositories.

## Test Steps
1. Add a few public repos (charmbracelet/gum, cli/cli)
2. Run ru sync
3. Verify repos are cloned
4. Run ru sync again
5. Verify repos show as 'current'
6. Manually modify a repo
7. Run ru sync
8. Verify dirty detection or appropriate handling

## Note
This test requires network. May not run in all CI environments.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clone works for public repos
- Pull works for existing repos
- Status detection is accurate","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T20:22:58.234735256-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T20:22:58.234735256-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1504","depends_on_id":"bd-1001","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.250911827-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1504","depends_on_id":"bd-15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.221459343-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1505","title":"Final ShellCheck and polish pass","description":"**Final quality pass before release**

## What
Run ShellCheck, fix all warnings, verify consistency.

## Checks
1. ShellCheck with severity warning: 0 warnings
2. All help text matches actual behavior
3. All error messages are actionable
4. Terminology is consistent
5. No debug output left in
6. VERSION file matches script

## Polish Items
- Remove any TODO comments
- Verify all functions are documented
- Check for unused functions
- Verify exit codes are consistent

## Acceptance Criteria
- ShellCheck clean
- Ready for v1.0.0 release

 {"id":"bd-2","title":"Phase 2: Core Script Skeleton","description":"**EPIC: Main Script Architecture & Entry Point**

## Goal
Create the ru script skeleton with proper shell conventions, argument parsing, subcommand dispatch, and the foundational patterns that all features will build upon.

## Rationale
The architecture decisions made here ripple through the entire codebase. Getting the error handling pattern right (NOT using set -e globally), establishing stream separation (stderr for humans, stdout for data), and setting up proper exit traps prevents countless bugs later.

## Critical Patterns
- `set -uo pipefail` but NOT `set -e` - we handle errors explicitly
- EXIT trap for cleanup and guaranteed summary output
- Subcommand dispatch pattern allows future extensibility
- Global state variables clearly defined at top

## Why Not set -e?
The `set -e` trap is subtle: `output=$(failing_cmd); exit_code=$?` exits before capturing the exit code. We need repos to continue processing even when one fails, so explicit error handling is mandatory.

## Success Criteria
- Script runs without syntax errors
- `ru --help` and `ru --version` work
- Subcommand routing works (even if subcommands are stubs)
- EXIT trap fires and cleans up temp files","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:16:38.688364552-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:16:38.688364552-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2","depends_on_id":"bd-1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:12:12.031401748-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
